mnemonics.title: Unit Testing
mnemonics.content: Unit testing is like checking individual LEGO bricks before building a castle.

mnemonics.title: Integration Testing
mnemonics.content: Integration testing is like building a LEGO castle and checking if it stands tall.

mnemonics.title: Validation Testing
mnemonics.content: Validation testing is like checking if the LEGO castle meets the blueprint requirements.

acronyms.usage: TDD is a software development process where tests are written before the code.
BDD is a software development process where tests are written in a natural language style.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a single LEGO brick being tested for its shape, size, and color.
Imagine multiple LEGO bricks being connected to form a structure, and testing if it stands tall.
Imagine checking if the LEGO structure meets the blueprint requirements.

analogies.analogy: Unit testing is like checking individual ingredients before baking a cake.
Integration testing is like mixing the ingredients together and checking if the cake turns out right.
Validation testing is like checking if the cake meets the recipe requirements.
